statement.p: Simplify this expression, and if applicable, write the restricted domain.

solution.p: To simplify a complex fraction, we find the common denominator of all fractions. In this case, it is {\left(y+2\right)\mathopen{}\left(y-2\right)}. We multiply the complex fractions numerator and denominator by this common denominator, and we have:
Since y+2 and y-2 were canceled, y \ne 2 and y \ne -2 are lost domain conditions.
